title: Understanding the Problem
text: To find the time it takes for the roller coaster car to reach the bottom, we need to consider the forces acting on it and use the equations of motion.

title: Identify the Forces
text: Since friction is ignored, the only force acting on the car along the incline is the component of gravitational force. This force can be calculated using the formula: $$F=m g sin\left(\theta\right)$$, where $$m$$ is the mass, g is the acceleration due to gravity, and theta is the angle of the incline.

title: Calculate the Acceleration
text: The acceleration of the car can be found using the formula: $$a=g sin\left(\theta\right)$$. This is because the net force is $$m g sin\left(\theta\right)$$ and $$F=ma$$, so $$a=g sin\left(\theta\right)$$.

title: Use the Equation of Motion
text: To find the time, use the equation of motion: $$s=ut+0.5a t^2$$, where s is the distance, u is the initial velocity, a is the acceleration, and $$t$$ is the time. Since the car starts from rest, $$u=0$$.

title: Solve for Time
text: Rearrange the equation $$s=0.5a t^2$$ to solve for t: $$t=\sqrt{\frac{2s}{a}}$$.
